items.title: Dow Jones closes at record 51,999.67; Nasdaq sells off 1.15%
items.content: Dow hits second consecutive record close at 51,999.67, led by financial, transport and manufacturing sectors. Easing Middle East tensions and falling oil prices fueled rotation out of AI/tech into cyclicals. Nasdaq Composite closed lower by 1.15%, S&P 500 down 0.57%. Investors cautious ahead of Fed policy decision.

items.title: US and Iran reach ceasefire agreement, Strait of Hormuz to reopen
items.content: US and Iran signed MOU to extend ceasefire and reopen Strait of Hormuz. Formal signing scheduled June 19 in Switzerland, mediated by Pakistan. MOU includes immediate cessation of military operations, 60-day negotiation window on sanctions and nuclear program, and reopening of Strait of Hormuz. US to lift naval blockade within 30 days of signing.

items.title: Iran fires drones at commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire
items.content: Despite the ceasefire MOU, Iran launched drones targeting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz on June 17. U.S. military intercepted the drones. The incident raises questions about the durability of the truce ahead of the June 19 signing ceremony.

items.title: FOMC June meeting underway—rate decision expected June 18
items.content: FOMC began two-day meeting June 16-17, with rate decision and Summary of Economic Projections to be released. Markets broadly expect rates held steady, but dot plot and Powell's guidance on inflation trajectory will be key. April PCE at 3.8% headline remains well above target.

items.title: Hang Seng Index at 24,494—old economy surging, Chinese internet stocks plunging
items.content: HSI mixed at ~24,494. Techtronic Industries +20% on buyback program. Orient Overseas +17%, Sun Hung Kai Properties +16%. Meanwhile Kuaishou -22% on analyst forecast cuts, Meituan, Alibaba and Tencent all down double digits. Hang Seng Tech Index fell 0.46% to 4,636. HK50 down 4.88% over past month.

items.title: US April PCE inflation at 3.8% YoY headline, 3.3% core
items.content: BEA reported April PCE at 3.8% YoY headline, core 3.3% YoY. Both remain elevated and above Fed's 2% target. Next release for May PCE scheduled June 25. Data reinforces Fed's cautious stance on rate cuts.

items.title: China semiconductor/AI surge: IC exports +83.7% YoY, DeepSeek nears $7B raise
items.content: China's integrated circuit exports surged 83.7% YoY in first 4 months of 2026. Major indices rebalancing to add more AI/semiconductor names. AI startup DeepSeek close to finalizing ~$7B funding round. US held off blacklisting DeepSeek to avoid escalating tensions. Cambricon planning major AI chip production ramp.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 beat: EPS $1.28 vs $1.23 est, revenue $8.94B, comps +2%
items.content: Best Buy reported Q1 FY27 EPS of $1.28, above $1.23 consensus. Revenue $8.94B (+1.9% YoY) beat $8.82B estimate. Comparable sales turned positive at +2%, driven by gaming, computing, mobile phones and services. Appliances declined.

items.title: Dollar Tree Q1 beat: adj EPS $1.74 (+38% YoY), revenue $4.98B, raises FY outlook
items.content: Dollar Tree Q1 FY26 adj EPS $1.74, +38% YoY. Revenue $4.98B (+7.2%). Comps +3.5%. Raised FY26 adj EPS guidance to $6.70-$7.10. Plans to open ~400 new Dollar Tree stores in FY26 while closing 75.

items.title: Hong Kong Q1 GDP +5.9% YoY, unemployment steady at 3.7%
items.content: Hong Kong economy expanded 5.9% YoY in Q1 2026, accelerating from 4.0% prior quarter. Seasonally adjusted unemployment remained at 3.7% for March-May 2026. Full-year GDP forecast maintained at 2.5%-3.5%. Underlying inflation at 1.6% in April.

items.title: Taiwan considering restrictions on AI chip sales to China, aligning with US policy
items.content: Taiwan is considering new restrictions on AI chip sales to China, aligning with US policies and aiming to enhance enforcement capabilities. The move adds to ongoing tech supply chain tensions and could impact semiconductor names on both sides.
